review.summary: HD 93521 is a massive, rapidly rotating O9.5 IIInn star located ~1 kpc above the Galactic disk. The paper calculates a time-of-flight of 39+/-3 Myr from Gaia EDR3 astrometry and radial velocity measurements (Table 1), then derives stellar parameters via a rotational model fit to the SED (Table 2), yielding an evolutionary age of ~5+/-2 Myr. The large discrepancy is explained by a binary merger scenario: the system was ejected as a close binary of two ~8 Msun stars that later merged, rejuvenating the product. Two data tables present radial velocity compilation and stellar parameters.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Compilation of all historical and new radial velocity measurements for HD 93521 used to compute the error-weighted mean radial velocity (-8.9+/-2.9 km/s) for the time-of-flight calculation.

columns.name: Source
columns.meaning: Reference for the radial velocity measurements

columns.name: Number of Measurements
columns.meaning: Count of individual radial velocity spectra used in the average

columns.name: Mean Date (HJD-2400000)
columns.meaning: Mean Heliocentric Julian Date of the observations minus 2400000

columns.name: <V_r>
columns.meaning: Error-weighted or simple mean radial velocity

columns.name: sigma(E)
columns.meaning: External standard deviation between individual measurements (scatter)

columns.name: sigma(I)
columns.meaning: Average of the internal estimated errors for individual measurements

internal_tables.evidence: Table 1 compiles radial velocities from Plaskett (1931), Conti (1977), Garmany (1980), Howarth (1993), Fullerton (1996, two epochs), Cazorla (2017), plus new measurements from IUE (139 spectra) and ESPaDOnS (5 spectra).
internal_tables.comments: The weighted mean of all entries gives <V_r> = -8.9 +/- 2.9 km/s. Some entries lack a mean date (nodata) or internal errors (nodata).

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Comparison of stellar parameters for HD 93521 derived from the rotational model in this paper versus the earlier study by Howarth & Smith (2001). Provides the key physical parameters used to place the star on the H-R diagram and determine its evolutionary age.

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Physical quantity describing the star or model

columns.name: Howarth & Smith (2001)
columns.meaning: Parameter value from the earlier study by Howarth & Smith (2001) using von Zeipel gravity darkening and a distance of 2.2 kpc

columns.name: This Paper
columns.meaning: Parameter value from this work using omega-model gravity darkening, line-blanketed TLUSTY atmospheres, and Gaia EDR3 distance of 1.246 kpc

internal_tables.evidence: Table 2 lists 18 parameters: gravity darkening law, v_e sin i, inclination i, equatorial velocity v_e, critical velocity v_c, Omega/Omega_c, polar radius R_p, equatorial radius R_e, mass M, log g_p, log g_e, log g(avg), T_p, T_e, <T>(avg), <T>(all), log(L/Lsun), He/H ratio y, and distance d.
internal_tables.comments: Key results: R_p=6.1 Rsun, M=17 Msun, log(L/Lsun)=4.6, <T>(all)=31.0 kK. The revised distance from Gaia EDR3 (1.246 kpc vs old 2.2 kpc) drives significantly lower radius and luminosity.

external_resources.description: PolarBase archive of high-resolution spectropolarimetric data. ESPaDOnS spectra of HD 93521 used for radial velocity measurements were obtained from this archive.
external_resources.evidence: ESPaDOnS spectra retrieved from PolarBase archive; 5 high S/N, high resolution spectra used for radial velocity cross-correlation with TLUSTY model template.

external_resources.description: HST CALSPEC spectral flux standard database. HD 93521 is a CALSPEC standard star; its UV+optical SED from IUE and HST/STIS was used for angular diameter fitting and SED analysis.

source_refs.context:
optical part of the spectrum is well established thanks to its 
inclusion as a spectral flux standard in the Hubble Space Telescope 
(HST) CALSPEC\footnote{https://www.stsci.edu/hst/instrumentation/reference-data-for-calibration-and-tools/astronomical-catalogs/calspec} 
program \citep{Bohlin2014, Bohlin2020}.

external_resources.evidence: Observed SED from IUE (1148-1680 A) and HST/STIS (1680-10120 A) taken from CALSPEC; rebinned to R=500 for comparison with TLUSTY model SED.
